title: 7 AFT Deadlines and Record Entry Limits
body_text:
7	AFT Deadlines and Record Entry Limits
7.1	AFT File Delivery Deadlines
AFT files must be sent to Central 1 before the applicable deadline. See the following table for details:
Note:	Calculate your equivalent time based on the Pacific Time zone provided.
Service
Recommended File Delivery Deadline
Optional File Delivery Deadline
Contingency Situation â in any of the service options
3:00 pm local time (Lead Time still applies as noted below)
N/A
File Transmission â via File and Report Exchange
2:00 pm local time, two Business Days before the Due Date
One Business Day before Due Date**
by 3:00 pm local time.
PaymentStream
AFT File Upload
2:00 pm local time, two Business Days before the Due Date. The validation process on
PaymentStream
AFT is immediate upon the upload of a successful AFT file.
Note:
Files may be validated outside of business hours, but transactions are only delivered to financial institutions during business hours.
One Business Day before the Due Date
by 4:00 pm local time.
Important
:
If you choose to deliver AFT files by 4:00 pm local time, one day before the Due Date,
Central 1 may not have enough time to resolve AFT file errors.
AFT Originators will be unable to request a recall of a Credit Transaction or an AFT file recall from Central 1.
PaymentStream
AFT Manual Release
Recommendation:
2:00 pm local time, two Business Days before the Due Date. The validation process on
PaymentStream
AFT is immediate upon the release of transactions.
Note:
Transactions may be validated outside of business hours, but transactions are only delivered to financial institutions during business hours.
One Business Day before the Due Date
by
4:00 pm local time.
Important
:
If you choose to release transactions by 4:00 pm local time, one day before the Due Date,
Central 1 may not have enough time to resolve AFT errors.
AFT Originators will be unable to request a recall of a Credit Transaction or an AFT file from Central 1.
PaymentStream
AFT Automatic Release
AFT transactions are automatically released three Business Days before the Due Date at the following intervals:
2:00 am PT,
4:00 am PT,
7:00 am PT,
11:00 am PT,
1:30 pm PT,
3:00 pm PT, and
4:00 pm PT.
N/A
*An exception can occur when the Serviceability Code (as defined in the Financial Institutions File) is more than one day.
**Important
: If you choose to deliver AFT files by the later optional delivery time, note the following:
The destination financial institutions must have CPA Serviceability Code â0â or â1.â
A later file delivery may decrease the ability for Central 1 to resolve AFT file errors (e.g., incorrect file creation number, exceeds file dollar limits).
Financial institutions will be unable to request a recall of a Credit Transaction or an AFT file recall from Central 1.
7.2	Record Entry Limits
The Record Entry Limit is the Due Date of an AFT Transaction that can be backdated or future-dated. See the tables below:
Backdated AFT Transactions
Debit Records
Credit Records
Applicable to every release type
PADs can be backdated up to
173
calendar days*
Direct Deposits can be backdated up to
30
calendar days*
Future-dated AFT Transactions
Debit Records
Credit Records
Data Entry Manual Release
PADs can be future-dated up to
14
calendar days*
Direct Deposits can be future-dated up to
14
calendar
days*
Data Entry Automatic Release
Future-dated record entry limits for automatic release types arenât applicable as the system extracts transactions three business days prior to the due date.
Future-dated record entry limits for automatic release types arenât applicable as the system extracts transactions three business days prior to the due date.
File Upload Release
PADs can be future-dated up to
45
calendar days*
Direct Deposits can be future-dated up to
45
calendar days*
*The time limit calculation is based on the date the record is created or modified in
PaymentStream
AFT. If an AFT file is uploaded to
PaymentStream
AFT, the time limit calculation is based on the file creation date.
END
